# NotebookTools

> NotebookTools is a free Chrome extension that imports YouTube videos, X (Twitter) posts, webpages, and selected text directly into Google NotebookLM. Right-click any highlight to add it.

NotebookTools helps researchers capture web content into NotebookLM without copy-paste. Uses your existing Google sign-in.

## What it does

- **YouTube**: Import button on watch pages. One click to add a video source.
- **X / Twitter**: Import threads and articles as text sources.
- **Webpages**: Import any page URL from the extension popup.
- **Selected text**: Highlight text, right-click, choose **Add to NotebookLM**.

## Key facts

- **Price**: Free
- **Platform**: Google Chrome (Manifest V3)
- **Auth**: Your signed-in NotebookLM session
- **Affiliation**: Independent — not affiliated with Google

## Pages

- Home: https://notebooktools.vercel.app/
- Support: https://notebooktools.vercel.app/support
- Privacy: https://notebooktools.vercel.app/privacy

## Common questions

**How do I add highlighted text to NotebookLM?**
Select text on any page, right-click, choose Add to NotebookLM, pick a notebook.

**How do I import a YouTube video?**
Visit a watch page, click the NotebookLM button, choose a notebook.
